family_name: Prime labelings of zero-divisor graph families
strongest_honest_claim: The strongest honest claim after this Lean pass is no longer purely prospective: for odd prime p, the F2 line now has a checked ring-level support partition bridge, namely that the genuine nonzero zero-divisor vertices of Z_p × Z_p × Z_2 land exactly in the six support classes A,B,C,D,E,F already used by the abstract reduction surface. The paired family slice is still only a candidate, not exact, because the F2 adjacency table and the full F25 partition/adjacency bridge are still missing in Lean, and the four-class arithmetic has not yet cleared the first post-p = 13 stress test at Γ(Z_17 × Z_25).
paper_title_hint: Ring-to-support reductions and paired p = 13 checkpoints in two zero-divisor prime-labeling families
theorem_slice_target: Extend the checked F2 ring-level support partition lemma to the exact F2 adjacency bridge, then formalize the matching F25 partition/adjacency bridge so the preserved abstract support-template reductions become honest family wrapper theorems rather than campaign prose.
fallback_target: If the remaining adjacency bridge or the F25 bridge does not close cleanly, preserve the checked F2 partition bridge together with the abstract reductions and the paired p = 13 checkpoints, and isolate the smallest still-unformalized ring-to-support step instead of claiming a full paired family slice.
next_blocker: The immediate Lean blocker is the missing F2 ring-level adjacency table for the exact edge families A-B, A-C, A-F, B-C, B-E, and C-D. After that, the four-class F25 partition/adjacency bridge is still missing, and only then does the next arithmetic pressure point return to Γ(Z_17 × Z_25).
